rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical bromocyclization of N-tosyl alkynyl alkenes mediated by an aryl iodide catalyst to form bromo-substituted pyrrolidine/heterocyclic products.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of [C2MPr]Br (ammonium bromide electrolyte) occurs first (Eox ≈ 1.21 V) to generate Br radical species; the aryl iodide (p-Tol-I) likely acts as a redox mediator. Cathode reduces protons/solvent.

key_intermediates.name: bromine radical (Br•) from anodic oxidation of bromide
carbon-centered radical from Br• addition to alkene/alkyne of substrate 1a
hypervalent aryl-iodine species from p-Tol-I oxidation (proposed mediator)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes bromide (and/or aryl iodide mediator) to generate a Br-centered radical, which adds to the alkyne/alkene of substrate 1a; the resulting carbon radical undergoes intramolecular cyclization and is terminated by a second bromine atom transfer to give the dibrominated heterocycle.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 10 equiv DPE under standard conditions completely suppressed formation of product 2a (N.R.), consistent with a free-radical pathway.

notes: CV shows oxidation of [C2MPr]Br (~1.21 V) is easier than that of 4-iodotoluene (~2.09 V) and substrate 1a, indicating bromide is oxidized first at the anode; the aryl iodide may still act as a redox mediator under turnover conditions. Radical trapping with DPE is suggestive but, as the authors note, inconclusive on its own.
